entries.definition:
d–m–d–m anger, to shake up violently; to inflict great punishment; to bury; to smother. Of this root, دَمْدَمَ damdama occurs once in the Qurʾan.
دَمْدَمَ damdama [quadriliteral v., intrans. with prep. عَلَى] to crush, to destroy, to visit with great punishment (91:14) فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِذَنبِهِمْ فَسَوَّاهَا but they called him a liar and hamstrung her, so their Lord crushed them for their sin and flattened it [their town].

d–m–d–m anger, to shake up violently; to inflict great punishment; to bury; to smother. Of this root, دَمْدَمَ damdama occurs once in the Qurʾan.
دَمْدَمَ damdama [quadriliteral v., intrans. with prep. عَلَى] to crush, to destroy, to visit with great punishment (91:14) فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِذَنبِهِمْ فَسَوَّاهَا but they called him a liar and hamstrung her, so their Lord crushed them for their sin and flattened it [their town].
